title: The Year Davos Stopped Smiling Sterile
summary: The 2025 World Economic Forum in Davos marked a shift from technocratic optimism to cold pragmatism, with Mark Carney and Emmanuel Macron delivering manifestos for a new era of sovereignty. The author argues Romania must prioritize EU integration as its primary strategic alliance, build a European defense pillar within NATO, and treat the US relationship as a mature partnership rather than a dependency.
talking_points: Mark Carney declared the end of the rules-based international order, calling it a 'useful fiction,' and urged countries to stop pretending global integration is mutually beneficial when great powers use it as a tool of subordination
Emmanuel Macron called for European strategic sovereignty, rejecting vassalization and demanding Europe stop being naive while other players aggressively protect their markets
Romania must reorder its alliance priorities: EU first as the only structure where it has voting rights, then NATO with a strong European defense pillar, and finally the US as a mature partnership
The author argues Romania's influence in Washington grows proportionally with its weight within the EU, not through bilateral dependency
Both leaders' message is a call for dignity: power begins with the honesty to name reality
author: Mihai Gabriel Costea
